sections.paragraph:
const. חֲדַר with suff. חֶדְרֹו, plur. חֲדָרִים const. חַדְרֵי m.

(1) a chamber, especially an inner apartment, whether of a tent or of a house, Gen 43:30 Jud. 16:9 , 12 Jdg 16:12hence a bed chamber, 2Sa 4:7 13:10 women’s apartment, Son 1:4 3:4 a bridal chamber, Jud. 15:1 Joe 2:16 a store room, Pro 24:4. (Arab. خِدْرُ a curtain by which an inner apartment is hidden, whence an inner apartment, a private apartment, compare יְרִיעָה the curtain of a tent, and Syr. ܝܪܳܝܺܥܐܳ a tent.)

(2) metaph. חַדְרֵי־תֵמָן Job 9:9, the chambers of the south, the most remote southern regions, comp. יַרְבְּתֵי צָפֹון.-חַדְרֵי־בֶמֶן the innermost parts of the breast, Pro 18:8 26:22 . מַדְרֵי־מָוֶת “the chambers of death,” i.e. of Hades.

[חֲדַר Hadar, pr.n., Gen 25:15.]
